_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**1. Carrier Carbon-Cost Transparency Infrastructure**
Scope: All air, road, and ocean carrier contracts on EU, UK, and Japan lanes.
Requirement: The workspace must build or formalise a process to collect, validate, and record ETS-driven surcharge data from each carrier — including the regulatory basis (EU ETS, UK ETS, GX-ETS), the per-tonne allowance cost, the calculation methodology, and the route or activity scope. For air freight on EU and UK lanes, this is immediately required given the 2026 full phase-out of free allocation for aviation in both systems. For ocean freight on UK domestic routes, this must be operational before 1 July 2026.
Gap from baseline: Most freight forwarders have ad hoc surcharge-acceptance processes; a structured, auditable carbon-cost data collection layer with carrier-level documentation is typically absent.
Deadline: EU/UK aviation: immediate (in force). UK maritime: 1 July 2026.

**2. Scope 3 Category 4 Emissions Reporting — Carrier ETS Alignment**
Scope: All transport modes; all trade lanes.
Requirement: The workspace should integrate ETS-verified emissions data from carriers into its Scope 3 emissions accounting. As ETS-covered carriers produce annual verified emissions reports, these become a high-integrity data source for Scope 3 Category 4 (upstream transportation and distribution). The workspace should establish data-sharing agreements or contractual data-provision clauses with covered carriers.
Gap from baseline: Scope 3 reporting at many freight forwarders relies on spend-based or distance-based emission factors rather than carrier-verified ETS data.
Deadline: For 2026 reporting cycles, begin now.

**3. Carrier Onboarding — ETS Registration and Compliance Status**
Scope: Japan GX-ETS (aviation, logistics); UK ETS maritime (domestic ship operators); EU ETS (aircraft operators, shipping companies).
Requirement: The workspace must add ETS registration status fields to its carrier onboarding and supplier qualification process. Carriers should be asked to confirm: (a) whether they are a regulated entity under applicable ETS; (b) their registry ID or permit number; (c) their allowance procurement and surrender schedule; (d) their verified emissions for the most recent compliance period.
For Japan GX-ETS, benchmark formulas for aviation and shipping are still being finalised by MLIT; the workspace should set a monitoring trigger to update carrier records when the MLIT rules are published.
Gap from baseline: Standard carrier onboarding does not typically include ETS compliance status.
Deadline: UK maritime entry: 1 July 2026. Japan GX-ETS mandatory period: April 2026 onward.

**4. Contract Clause Review — Carbon Cost Pass-Through**
Scope: Air-freight and ocean-freight master agreements; spot booking terms on EU, UK, and Japan lanes.
Requirement: Carrier agreements should include provisions defining how ETS-driven carbon costs are calculated, disclosed, and passed through — and whether such costs can be varied mid-contract following allowance price changes. Client-facing agreements should include equivalent transparency clauses so that carbon surcharges are separately itemised, not bundled into opaque fuel surcharges.
Gap from baseline: Legacy freight agreements typically address only fuel surcharges; ETS cost pass-through is often handled via carrier tariff amendments without contractual notice requirements.
Deadline: Immediate for EU and UK aviation; before 1 July 2026 for UK maritime.

**5. Staff Training — ETS Basics for Operations and Commercial Teams**
Scope: Operations, pricing, and commercial staff handling EU, UK, and Japan lanes.
Requirement: ICAP provides training courses and ETS Briefs covering ETS design and implementation. The workspace should utilise these resources to build baseline literacy among operations and commercial staff on: how cap-and-trade works; which carrier activities are covered in each jurisdiction; how allowance prices translate to cargo surcharges; and what documentation to request from carriers. ICAP training courses offer ten-day to two-week intensive programmes for those requiring deeper engagement.
Gap from baseline: ETS awareness is typically concentrated in compliance or sustainability functions and absent from front-line operations.
Deadline: Prior to 1 July 2026 UK maritime entry-into-force.

*Source: ICAP Status Report 2026, ICAP, 14 April 2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/publications/emissions-trading-worldwide-icap-status-report-2026. UK ETS Factsheet, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/uk-emissions-trading-scheme-uk-ets. Japan GX-ETS Factsheet, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/japan-gx-ets. ICAP Training Courses, Wikipedia/ICAP. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Carbon_Action_Partnership.*
